text: is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [0] 这里是美国之音慢速英语农业报道节目。

text: Compost is a dark, rich material added to soil to improve it.
zh: [1] 堆肥是一种深色、富含养分的物质,加入土壤中可以改良土质。

text: Compost is produced when bacteria and fungi
zh: [2] 堆肥是由细菌和真菌

text: break down organic matter, aided by insects, earthworms, and other organisms.
zh: [3] 在昆虫、蚯蚓和其他生物的帮助下分解有机物而产生的。

text: This natural recycling returns nutrients to the soil.
zh: [4] 这种自然循环过程能把养分归还给土壤。

text: Some experts advise adding 5 centimeters of compost
zh: [5] 一些专家建议,每翻耕15厘米的土壤,

text: for every 15 centimeters of turned soil.
zh: [6] 就添加5厘米厚的堆肥。

text: More and more people are making their own compost at home.
zh: [7] 越来越多的人开始在家自制堆肥。

text: Composting can reduce the amount of garden and cooking waste
zh: [8] 堆肥可以减少花园垃圾和厨余垃圾的数量,

text: that would otherwise go into public landfills.
zh: [9] 否则这些垃圾就会被送往公共垃圾填埋场。

text: Monica David from the University of Illinois Extension
zh: [10] 来自伊利诺伊大学推广中心的莫妮卡·戴维

text: oversees the Master Gardeners program in her state.
zh: [11] 负责该州的“园艺大师”项目。

text: She explains that you can make a compost pile,
zh: [12] 她解释说,你可以堆一个堆肥堆,

text: or dig a pit, or use a waste container with holes cut in the side.
zh: [13] 或者挖一个坑,或者使用一个侧面打了孔的垃圾桶。

text: There are different methods of composting. Some take more work.
zh: [14] 堆肥有不同的方法,有些比较费工夫。

text: Others take almost no work, but may require waiting up to a year.
zh: [15] 有些几乎不用费什么力气,但可能要等上一年时间。

text: Advice about composting can be found from extension services,
zh: [16] 关于堆肥的建议可以从推广服务机构、

text: at garden centers, in books, and on the internet.
zh: [17] 园艺中心、书籍以及互联网上找到。

text: For example, the New York State Department of Environmental Conservation
zh: [18] 例如,纽约州环境保护部

text: lists some do's and don'ts of composting on its website.
zh: [19] 就在其网站上列出了一些堆肥的注意事项。

text: site. Do add lime, small amounts of wood ashes,
zh: [20] 网站上写道:可以往堆肥堆里加入石灰、少量的木灰,

text: or crushed eggshells to the compost pile.
zh: [21] 或者压碎的蛋壳。

text: This will neutralize acids which may form and cause a bad smell.
zh: [22] 这样可以中和可能形成并产生异味的酸性物质。

text: Do mix grass cuttings with other wastes to loosen them up.
zh: [23] 可以把剪下的草与其他废物混合,使其更蓬松。

text: And do keep the compost pile damp, especially during dry periods.
zh: [24] 还要保持堆肥堆湿润,尤其是在干旱时期。

text: Do not use unfinished compost.
zh: [25] 不要使用未腐熟的堆肥。

text: It will rob plants of nitrogen instead of acting as a fertilizer.
zh: [26] 它不但不能起到肥料的作用,反而会争夺植物所需的氮。

text: Do not compost weedy plants with lots of seeds.
zh: [27] 不要把带有大量种子的杂草堆入堆肥。

text: Some seeds will not be killed during the natural heating process
zh: [28] 有些种子在堆肥的自然发热过程中不会被杀死。

text: in composting. Do Do not add meat, fish bones, or
zh: [29] 不要往堆肥混合物中加入肉类、鱼骨,或

text: fatty food waste to the compost mixture.
zh: [30] 油脂类的食物废料。

text: Protein materials do not readily decompose, and they will bring animals.
zh: [31] 蛋白质类物质不易分解,还会招来动物。

text: And finally, do not add diseased vegetable plants to the pile
zh: [32] 最后,如果堆肥将用于蔬菜园,

text: if the compost will be used on a vegetable garden.
zh: [33] 就不要把患病的蔬菜植株加入堆肥堆。

text: Disease organisms may reappear
zh: [34] 否则病原体可能会再次出现。

text: if you do. Compost differs from mulch, but it can be used as mulch.
zh: [35] 堆肥与覆盖物不同,但可以当作覆盖物使用。

text: is placed on top of soil to protect it.
zh: [36] 覆盖物铺在土壤表面以起到保护作用。

text: Mulch is generally used to a height of about 10 centimeters.
zh: [37] 覆盖物一般铺到约10厘米高。

text: But finer materials, such as compost,
zh: [38] 但像堆肥这类较细的材料,

text: should only be 2 1⁄2 to 7 1⁄2 centimeters high.
Too much mulch can restrict oxygen and water flow.
zh: [40] 覆盖物太厚会妨碍氧气和水分的流通。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: [41] 以上就是美国之音慢速英语农业报道节目。

text: For composting links, go to voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: [42] 有关堆肥的相关链接,请访问voaspecialenglish.com。
